rule_title: CI should not execute remote scripts without provenance checks
rule_summary: A pipeline step that downloads text from the network and immediately executes it as a shell script weakens provenance review.
rule_text: A pipeline step that downloads text from the network and immediately executes it as a shell script weakens provenance review.
fix_guidance: Split the flow into fetch, verify, and execute stages or add a pinned checksum verification step.

rule_title: FastAPI request payloads should be validated at the HTTP boundary
rule_summary: FastAPI works best when request payloads are validated through typed models at the HTTP boundary.
rule_text: FastAPI works best when request payloads are validated through typed models at the HTTP boundary.
fix_guidance: Prefer typed request models or an explicit validation step immediately after parsing.

rule_title: Blocking I/O should not run inside async FastAPI handlers
rule_summary: Async handlers are only cheap when their I/O model remains non-blocking.
rule_text: Async handlers are only cheap when their I/O model remains non-blocking.
fix_guidance: Move blocking work to a thread pool or use an async-native client.

rule_title: Avoid ordinal GROUP BY in committed warehouse and reporting queries
rule_summary: Ordinal grouping weakens reviewability and can silently change semantics.
rule_text: Group by explicit column names or aliases so grouping semantics remain stable.
fix_guidance: Use explicit column names or aliases in GROUP BY instead of positional ordinals.

rule_title: Async CUDA APIs that explicitly target stream 0 should justify default-stream ordering
rule_summary: Explicit stream 0 keeps async-looking code inside NULL-stream ordering semantics.
rule_text: An explicit 0 stream argument on cudaMemcpyAsync-style APIs often means the code still relies on default-stream ordering even though the call shape looks asynchronous.
fix_guidance: Route the work through an explicit owned stream when overlap matters, or document that NULL-stream ordering is the intended contract.

rule_title: Grid-wide cooperative-group synchronization should keep cooperative launch ownership explicit
rule_summary: `grid.sync()` is only valid when launch shape and residency assumptions are already owned by the caller.
rule_text: A `grid.sync()` call implies a cooperative launch contract that depends on grid size, residency, and launch API choices outside the local line.
fix_guidance: Keep the cooperative-launch requirement visible near the kernel launch or document the launch contract in the surrounding abstraction.
